The SOD1 protein is a crucial component of the mitochondrial electron transport chain (ETC) and plays a crucial role in the oxidative phosphorylation (OXPHOS) pathway.
The SOD1 protein is a crucial component of the mitochondrial electron transport chain (ETC) and plays a crucial role in the oxidative phosphorylation (OXPHOS) pathway.
Mutations in the SOD1 gene are the most common cause of familial ALS (fALS) and sporadic ALS (sALS).
The SOD1 gene encodes a superoxide dismutase (SOD) protein that catalyzes the conversion of superoxide anions (O2•−) into hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) and oxygen.

The SOD1 protein is a crucial component of the mitochondrial electron transport chain (ETC) and plays a crucial role in the oxidative phosphorylation (OXPHOS) pathway.
Mutations in the SOD1 gene are the most common cause of familial ALS (fALS) and sporadic ALS (sALS).
The SOD1 gene encodes a superoxide dismutase (SOD) protein that catalyzes the conversion of superoxide anions (O2•−) into hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) and oxygen.
